name: Chocolate Mousse (2)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine chocolate and coffee in a double boiler over almost-simmering water.
Stir until chocolate begins to melt.
2. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolks while gradually adding 3/4 cup sugar.
Beat for 2-3 minutes until the mixture is thick, pale, and forms a slowly
dissolving ribbon when the beater is lifted. Add the orange liqueur and
set aside in a pan of almost-simmering water.
3. In another bowl, beat the egg whites with salt until they begin to foam.
Gradually increase speed to fast and continue beating until soft peaks
form. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons sugar and beat until stiff peaks
form.
4. Stir the chocolate mixture until smooth, then gradually beat in butter.
Beat until warm and foamy, but not boiling.
5. Carefully fold the egg yolk mixture into the chocolate-butter mixture.
6. Gradually stir in one-fourth of the beaten egg whites into the chocolate
mixture, then gently fold in the remaining egg whites until well combined.
7. Spoon the mousse into a serving bowl, lightly oiled metal mold, or
individual cups. Cover and chill overnight.
8. Serve chilled with whipped cream if desired.
